questions.question: In `add_product`, what uniqueness check is performed before inserting a new product into `TBL_PRODUCTS_MASTER`?

options.text: SKU must be globally unique across all clients and all tables
SKU + brand combination must be unique within the same client
SKU must be unique within the same `client_id`
No uniqueness check is performed — duplicates are allowed

questions.question: In the `products` listing action, what type of JOIN is used for `TBL_PRODUCTS_CATEGORY`?

options.text: LEFT JOIN
INNER JOIN
RIGHT JOIN
FULL OUTER JOIN

questions.question: What is the image resize target dimension for master product images in the upload pipeline?

options.text: 100×100 pixels with aspect ratio preservation
500×500 pixels with fixed dimensions
1000×1000 pixels with aspect ratio preservation
Original dimensions — no resizing is performed

questions.question: In the `edit_product` deactivation guard, which inventory statuses are checked before allowing `is_active=0`?

options.text: Only `qc_verified` and `qc_pending`
`inbound`, `qc_cooling`, `qc_verified`, `qc_pending`, `qc_failed`, `components_requested`
All inventory statuses including `dispatched` and `returned`
No check is performed — products can always be deactivated

questions.question: In `delete_product`, what referential integrity checks are performed before deleting a product from `TBL_PRODUCTS_MASTER`?

options.text: It checks for active aliases in `TBL_PRODUCTS_ALIAS` and active combos in `TBL_PRODUCTS_COMBO`
It checks for active inventory in `TBL_INVENTORY` using the same guard as `edit_product`
It performs a soft delete by setting `is_active=0` instead of a hard DELETE
No checks — it executes a direct `$db->delete()` with only a `@TODO` comment about missing checks

questions.question: In `edit_product`, what condition must be TRUE for price changes (selling_price, retail_price, family_price, moq, lead_time) to be logged to `TBL_PRODUCTS_LOG`?

options.text: The user must have admin or manager role
The price must have increased — decreases are not logged
The product's `is_stock_offline` flag must be TRUE
The product's `in_stock` flag must be TRUE

questions.question: How does the `TBL_PRODUCTS_LOG` record distinguish between a price increase and a price decrease?

options.text: It stores both old and new values in separate columns
The `log_details` field contains either `'Up'` or `'Down'`
The `log_type` field is prefixed with `increase_` or `decrease_`
It uses a numeric `change_direction` column with values 1 or -1

questions.question: Which of the following field changes in `edit_product` is logged to `TBL_PRODUCTS_LOG` WITHOUT the `is_stock_offline` gating condition?

options.text: `selling_price` changes
`moq` changes
`in_stock_rtl` status changes
`family_price` changes

questions.question: What data type does `TBL_PRODUCTS_COMBO.pid` use, and how are inner product IDs stored?

options.text: `INT` — stores a single product ID representing the primary inner product
`VARCHAR(512)` — stores comma-separated product IDs like `"123,456"`
`longtext` — stores a JSON-encoded array of product ID strings like `["123","456"]`
A junction table `TBL_PRODUCTS_COMBO_ITEMS` is used instead of storing IDs in the combo table

questions.question: In the `products_alias` listing action, how many tables are JOINed together?

options.text: 3 tables (alias, master, category)
5 tables (alias, master, combo, category, one account table)
7 tables (alias, category, FK accounts, AZ accounts, master, combo — plus the base alias table)
9 tables (all product tables plus both account tables and Shopify products)

questions.question: In `add_product_alias`, how is the parent product type (master vs combo) determined from the form submission?

options.text: A separate `parent_type` POST field contains either `'master'` or `'combo'`
The `parent_sku` POST field uses a `pid-{id}` or `cid-{id}` prefix parsed via `explode('-', ...)`
The system queries both `TBL_PRODUCTS_MASTER` and `TBL_PRODUCTS_COMBO` by SKU to find a match
Master and combo aliases use separate AJAX actions (`add_master_alias` and `add_combo_alias`)

questions.question: In the `products_alias` listing query, what does `COALESCE(pc.sku, pm.sku)` resolve to for an alias linked to a master product (pid > 0, cid = 0)?

options.text: The combo SKU, because `pc.sku` takes precedence in `COALESCE`
The master product SKU, because the LEFT JOIN on combo returns NULL when `cid=0` and `COALESCE` falls through to `pm.sku`
NULL, because neither table matches
An error, because `COALESCE` cannot handle JOINed tables with conditional WHERE clauses

questions.question: In `update_stock`, what is the maximum stock count sent to Flipkart via the API?

options.text: 50 units
100 units — stock is capped with `if ($stock > 100) $stock = 100`
999 units — Flipkart API limit
No cap — the actual `qc_verified` count is sent directly

questions.question: How does `update_stock` find combo products that contain a specific product ID when checking stock status?

options.text: It queries a junction table `TBL_PRODUCTS_COMBO_ITEMS` with an exact match
It uses `WHERE pid LIKE '%{pid}%'` on `TBL_PRODUCTS_COMBO`, which may produce false positive matches
It uses `JSON_CONTAINS(pid, '"pid"')` for precise JSON array searching
It does not check combos — only direct product-to-alias mappings are updated

questions.question: In `edit_product`, what triggers the `$stockist->update_stock_on_mps('pid', $pid)` call to propagate stock changes to marketplaces?

options.text: Any field change on the product record
Changes to `selling_price` or `retail_price`
Changes to `committed_stock` or `amazon_reserved` (when new value > 0)
Changes to `in_stock` or `is_stock_offline` flags

questions.question: A user deletes category "Headphones" (catid=5) via the `delete_category` action. There are 12 products with `category=5` in `TBL_PRODUCTS_MASTER`. What happens to those 12 products?

options.text: They are automatically deleted via MySQL CASCADE
They remain in the database but display "Unknown Category" in the listing
They silently disappear from the product listing because the listing query uses INNER JOIN on `TBL_PRODUCTS_CATEGORY`
The delete fails due to a foreign key constraint violation

questions.question: In `update_product_bulk`, how are brand, model, and category names from the uploaded Excel file resolved to their database IDs?

options.text: Individual DB queries per row per field — one `getOne()` call per brand/model/category per row
Pre-fetched mapping arrays (`brandMap`, `modelMap`, `categoryMap`) built before the row loop using `array_column()` for name→ID lookup
The system expects brand/model/category IDs (not names) in the Excel file
A single raw SQL query with `CASE WHEN` statements handles all name resolution

questions.question: In the `add_product_brand` action, what is the error message displayed when a duplicate brand name is detected?

options.text: `"Duplicate Brand Name"`
`"Duplicate Category Name"` — a copy-paste error from the category code
`"Brand already exists"`
`"Error adding new product"` — the generic DB error message

questions.question: How many downstream modules depend on the Products module by reading from its tables?

options.text: 5 modules (Flipkart, Amazon, Shopify, Inventory, Purchase)
9 modules (all marketplace modules plus Inventory, Purchase, QAQC, Sales)
14 modules (Amazon, Flipkart, Shopify, Inventory, Purchase, QAQC, RMA, Sales, POS, Paytm, Distributor, AMS, Brand Authorization, Tools)
All 27 modules in the system

questions.question: What percentage of the 33 AJAX actions in the Products module have RBAC (role-based access control) checks?

options.text: 0% — no action checks user roles before execution
15% — only delete actions are protected
50% — write actions are protected, read actions are open
100% — all actions verify `access_level` before execution
